title: dodecatheon hendersonii subsp. hansenii
intro: Dodecatheon hendersonii subsp. hansenii, also known as the Henderson's shooting star, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant that belongs to the primrose family. It is native to the Pacific coastal regions of North America and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, soothing respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. Dodecatheon hendersonii subsp. hansenii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and colds.
